title: Reaffirming the Twenty-second Amendment.
plain_summary: This resolution reminds everyone that the Constitution says presidents can only serve two terms total. It points out that President Trump has already served one full term as president. The resolution lists several times when Trump suggested he might try to stay in office longer than the law allows. It makes clear that because of the Twenty-second Amendment to the Constitution, Trump cannot run for president again since he would exceed the two-term limit.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Reaffirming the Twenty-second Amendment
sections.text: This is a resolution from the House of Representatives that states the Twenty-second Amendment to the Constitution still applies. The Twenty-second Amendment is a rule that says a person can only serve as President of the United States for two terms. The resolution lists many times when President Trump talked about maybe staying in office longer than two terms or serving more than the allowed time. These comments happened between two thousand eighteen and two thousand twenty-five. The resolution ends by saying two things. First, it confirms that the Twenty-second Amendment means a person can only serve two terms total as President. Second, it confirms that this amendment stops President Trump from running for President again because he has already served two terms.
